name: Cross-artifact consistency — same sources, different formats

setup.description: Three papers (DAPA-HF, EMPEROR-Reduced, DELIVER) with 6 chunks. Tests cross-artifact consistency: a study guide and a FAQ generated from the same sources must contain the same HR values (0.74, 0.75, 0.79), cite the same papers, and identify DELIVER as a preserved EF population. Neither artifact may fabricate renal or kidney data.

papers.title: DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Heart Failure and Reduced Ejection Fraction
EMPEROR-Reduced: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
DELIVER: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with a Preserved Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.74; 95% CI 0.65-0.85; P<0.001). The number needed to treat was 21 over a median follow-up of 18.2 months.
Enrolled patients were aged ≥18 years with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40%. Patients were randomized 1:1 to dapagliflozin 10mg or placebo.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.65-0.86; P<0.001). The effect was consistent across prespecified subgroups.
Empagliflozin did NOT significantly reduce cardiovascular death alone (HR 0.92; P=0.23) or all-cause mortality (HR 0.92; P=0.22). 3730 patients were randomized with a median follow-up of 16 months.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure in patients with heart failure and a preserved ejection fraction (HR 0.79; 95% CI 0.69-0.90; P<0.001).
6263 patients with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF >40% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 2.2 years. Unlike HFrEF trials, DELIVER enrolled patients with preserved ejection fraction.

queries.query: Create a study guide covering all three heart failure trials
expectedBehavior: Has all five study guide sections
Cites [1] for DAPA-HF, [3] for EMPEROR-Reduced, [5] for DELIVER
failurePatterns: Missing one of the three HR values
Fails to note DELIVER population difference

queries.query: Generate a FAQ about these three SGLT2 inhibitor trials
expectedBehavior: Contains Q&A pairs with cited answers
CV death non-significance for EMPEROR-Reduced mentioned
failurePatterns: Missing one of the three trials
Does not address DELIVER population difference

qualityCriteria: Study guide has all 5 sections and covers all 3 trials
FAQ has Q&A pairs covering all 3 trials
HR values (0.74, 0.75, 0.79) are consistent between study guide and FAQ
DELIVER identified as preserved EF in both artifacts
Both artifacts cite all three papers' results chunks
No fabricated renal, kidney, cancer, or stroke content
